item.title: Version 1.1.0
item.description: Add ElevenLabs as second Read Aloud provider, audio input device selection, and reliability improvements

item.title: Version 1.1.1
item.description: API keys now persist across app restarts — no manual .env file needed

item.title: Version 2.0.0
item.description: Meeting notetaker: real-time transcription, AI summaries, floating indicator. Developer ID signed with hardened runtime. New app icon.

item.title: Version 2.0.2
item.description: Fix UI freezes on Intel Macs caused by IOKit HID device enumeration blocking the main thread. Moved HID monitor to dedicated background thread. Added native macOS toolbar for meeting window navigation. UI polish and improvements.

item.title: Version 2.0.3
item.description: Fix meeting transcript freezing mid-recording. WebSocket reconnection had a race condition that silently killed the audio stream — transcript would stop updating while audio kept recording. Now reconnects reliably.

item.title: Version 2.1.0
item.description: Switch Hebrew meeting transcription to Soniox for real-time streaming with speaker diarization. Fix memory leak and deadlocks in meeting recording.

item.title: Version 2.1.1
item.description: Fix app freeze when network is interrupted during meeting recording. Live transcription now resumes automatically when the connection returns.

item.title: Version 2.2.0
item.description:
Meeting Polish (new)

After every English meeting ends, Vord automatically re-transcribes via AssemblyAI's Universal-3 Pro for higher word accuracy and proper speaker diarization. Streaming diarization can struggle to distinguish voices reliably; polish gets it right. Toggle between Polished and Original in the meeting detail view.

Speaker names are auto-detected from conversation context when possible. Click any speaker badge to rename manually.

Multi-language polish

Hebrew, Hindi, and 90+ other languages get the same polish treatment via AssemblyAI Universal-2. Code-switching (English+Hebrew, English+Hindi, etc.) is preserved.

Auto-generated meeting titles

Vord now suggests a short, descriptive title for each meeting based on the summary. Manually rename anytime; your edit always wins, and auto-titles never overwrite manual changes.

Native-feel title rename

Click the meeting title to edit, click anywhere else (or press Enter) to save. Escape reverts. Hover the title for a pencil hint.

Improvements

  • "Polished" badge in the meeting library for processed sessions
  • Markdown export now includes the polished transcript when available
  • Regenerating summary from a polished transcript uses GPT-5.5 for higher-quality output
  • Speaker rename UI in the polished view

Bug fixes

  • System audio static: recordings with system audio (remote callers via Zoom, Meet, etc.) no longer have static or robotic distortion. Was a stereo to mono downmix clipping issue.
  • WebSocket livelock: long network outages during meetings no longer cause the app to hang.

Apple notarization

Vord is now Apple-notarized. Fresh installs and updates no longer trigger "unidentified developer" warnings, and macOS permissions (Accessibility, Input Monitoring) stick on the first grant.

item.title: Version 2.3.0
item.description:
What's new in 2.3.0

Polish now works on long meetings. Recordings over ~50 minutes were silently failing. Fixed.

Smarter speaker names. Vord picks up names from how people address each other in conversation, not just explicit self-introductions. Multi-speaker accuracy is dramatically better.

Better dark-mode handling. Vord stays in light mode regardless of your macOS appearance setting. (Proper dark mode coming later.)

Right Option as a backup hotkey. Hold Right Option (⌥) to record if Globe/Fn isn't your thing. Right Option + Space locks recording, same as Fn + Space.

Fixed

  • Force-quit recovery now keeps audio attached to the recovered meeting — Polish stays available instead of being permanently disabled.
  • Cloud connection status is real now: only shows \"Connected\" after a successful test, not just because the field has text in it.

If you missed 2.2.0

Meeting Polish (new)

After every English meeting ends, Vord automatically re-transcribes via AssemblyAI's Universal-3 Pro for higher word accuracy and proper speaker diarization. Streaming diarization can struggle to distinguish voices reliably; polish gets it right. Toggle between Polished and Original in the meeting detail view.

Speaker names are auto-detected from conversation context when possible. Click any speaker badge to rename manually.

Multi-language polish

Hebrew, Hindi, and 90+ other languages get the same polish treatment via AssemblyAI Universal-2. Code-switching (English+Hebrew, English+Hindi, etc.) is preserved.

Auto-generated meeting titles

Vord now suggests a short, descriptive title for each meeting based on the summary. Manually rename anytime; your edit always wins, and auto-titles never overwrite manual changes.

Native-feel title rename

Click the meeting title to edit, click anywhere else (or press Enter) to save. Escape reverts. Hover the title for a pencil hint.

Improvements

  • \"Polished\" badge in the meeting library for processed sessions
  • Markdown export now includes the polished transcript when available
  • Regenerating summary from a polished transcript uses GPT-5.5 for higher-quality output
  • Speaker rename UI in the polished view

Bug fixes

  • System audio static: recordings with system audio (remote callers via Zoom, Meet, etc.) no longer have static or robotic distortion.
  • WebSocket livelock: long network outages during meetings no longer cause the app to hang.

Apple notarization

Vord is now Apple-notarized. Fresh installs and updates no longer trigger \"unidentified developer\" warnings, and macOS permissions (Accessibility, Input Monitoring) stick on the first grant.

item.title: Version 2.3.1
item.description:
What's new in 2.3.0

Polish now works on long meetings. Recordings over ~50 minutes were silently failing. Fixed.

Smarter speaker names. Vord picks up names from how people address each other in conversation, not just explicit self-introductions. Multi-speaker accuracy is dramatically better.

Better dark-mode handling. Vord stays in light mode regardless of your macOS appearance setting. (Proper dark mode coming later.)

Right Option as a backup hotkey. Hold Right Option (⌥) to record if Globe/Fn isn't your thing. Right Option + Space locks recording, same as Fn + Space.

Fixed

  • Force-quit recovery now keeps audio attached to the recovered meeting — Polish stays available instead of being permanently disabled.
  • Cloud connection status is real now: only shows \"Connected\" after a successful test, not just because the field has text in it.

If you missed 2.2.0

Meeting Polish (new)

After every English meeting ends, Vord automatically re-transcribes via AssemblyAI's Universal-3 Pro for higher word accuracy and proper speaker diarization. Streaming diarization can struggle to distinguish voices reliably; polish gets it right. Toggle between Polished and Original in the meeting detail view.

Speaker names are auto-detected from conversation context when possible. Click any speaker badge to rename manually.

Multi-language polish

Hebrew, Hindi, and 90+ other languages get the same polish treatment via AssemblyAI Universal-2. Code-switching (English+Hebrew, English+Hindi, etc.) is preserved.

Auto-generated meeting titles

Vord now suggests a short, descriptive title for each meeting based on the summary. Manually rename anytime; your edit always wins, and auto-titles never overwrite manual changes.

Native-feel title rename

Click the meeting title to edit, click anywhere else (or press Enter) to save. Escape reverts. Hover the title for a pencil hint.

Improvements

  • \"Polished\" badge in the meeting library for processed sessions
  • Markdown export now includes the polished transcript when available
  • Regenerating summary from a polished transcript uses GPT-5.5 for higher-quality output
  • Speaker rename UI in the polished view

Bug fixes

  • System audio static: recordings with system audio (remote callers via Zoom, Meet, etc.) no longer have static or robotic distortion.
  • WebSocket livelock: long network outages during meetings no longer cause the app to hang.

Apple notarization

Vord is now Apple-notarized. Fresh installs and updates no longer trigger \"unidentified developer\" warnings, and macOS permissions (Accessibility, Input Monitoring) stick on the first grant.

item.title: Version 2.3.2
item.description:
What's new in 2.3.0

Polish now works on long meetings. Recordings over ~50 minutes were silently failing. Fixed.

Smarter speaker names. Vord picks up names from how people address each other in conversation, not just explicit self-introductions. Multi-speaker accuracy is dramatically better.

Better dark-mode handling. Vord stays in light mode regardless of your macOS appearance setting. (Proper dark mode coming later.)

Right Option as a backup hotkey. Hold Right Option (⌥) to record if Globe/Fn isn't your thing. Right Option + Space locks recording, same as Fn + Space.

Fixed

  • Force-quit recovery now keeps audio attached to the recovered meeting — Polish stays available instead of being permanently disabled.
  • Cloud connection status is real now: only shows "Connected" after a successful test, not just because the field has text in it.

If you missed 2.2.0

Meeting Polish (new)

After every English meeting ends, Vord automatically re-transcribes via AssemblyAI's Universal-3 Pro for higher word accuracy and proper speaker diarization. Streaming diarization can struggle to distinguish voices reliably; polish gets it right. Toggle between Polished and Original in the meeting detail view.

Speaker names are auto-detected from conversation context when possible. Click any speaker badge to rename manually.

Multi-language polish

Hebrew, Hindi, and 90+ other languages get the same polish treatment via AssemblyAI Universal-2. Code-switching (English+Hebrew, English+Hindi, etc.) is preserved.

Auto-generated meeting titles

Vord now suggests a short, descriptive title for each meeting based on the summary. Manually rename anytime; your edit always wins, and auto-titles never overwrite manual changes.

Native-feel title rename

Click the meeting title to edit, click anywhere else (or press Enter) to save. Escape reverts. Hover the title for a pencil hint.

Improvements

  • "Polished" badge in the meeting library for processed sessions
  • Markdown export now includes the polished transcript when available
  • Regenerating summary from a polished transcript uses GPT-5.5 for higher-quality output
  • Speaker rename UI in the polished view

Bug fixes

  • System audio static: recordings with system audio (remote callers via Zoom, Meet, etc.) no longer have static or robotic distortion.
  • WebSocket livelock: long network outages during meetings no longer cause the app to hang.

Apple notarization

Vord is now Apple-notarized. Fresh installs and updates no longer trigger "unidentified developer" warnings, and macOS permissions (Accessibility, Input Monitoring) stick on the first grant.

item.title: Version 2.4.0
item.description:
New: Dictation Cleanup (opt-in)

Enable in Settings → Text → "Clean up dictation output". Sends your raw transcript through GPT-4.1 to remove fillers, fix punctuation, and add paragraph breaks. Preserves your wording (no rephrasing). Off by default.

  • STT-agnostic: works with Deepgram (English) and Soniox (Hebrew + 60 languages)
  • Skipped automatically in code editors (Xcode, VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains, Sublime, Zed) and password managers (1Password, Bitwarden)
  • Skipped on dictations under 10 words (instant paste for quick messages)
  • Hebrew-aware: routes Hebrew dictation through a Hebrew prompt with bidi-correct paste of mixed Hebrew+English
  • Self-correction: say "scratch that", "nevermind", "no wait" mid-sentence and the wrong part is dropped
  • Failure falls back to raw transcript: never blocks a paste

Fixes

  • Hebrew meeting transcripts no longer get incorrectly re-polished by AssemblyAI (which has 4x worse Hebrew WER than Soniox)
